name: United Arab Emirates
overview: The UAE's federal Personal Data Protection Law (Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021) is a GDPR-influenced framework, though its Executive Regulations and the operationalisation of the UAE Data Office have been long delayed, limiting practical enforcement. The financial free zones run mature standalone regimes: DIFC Data Protection Law No. 5 of 2020 and ADGM Data Protection Regulations 2021, both actively enforced with adequacy-style transfer lists.

regulator.name: UAE Data Office (federal); DIFC and ADGM Commissioners of Data Protection in the free zones

legal_instruments.title: Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on the Protection of Personal Data (PDPL)
legal_instruments.summary: Federal law with GDPR-style concepts: consent-centric lawful bases, data subject rights, controller/processor duties, breach notification to the Data Office, and cross-border transfer conditions. Practical enforcement awaits Executive Regulations; penalties to be set by Cabinet decision.

legal_instruments.title: DIFC Law No. 5 of 2020 — Data Protection Law
legal_instruments.summary: GDPR-aligned free-zone law with lawful bases, DPO requirements for high-risk processing, breach notification, adequacy list for exports, and fines (general cap USD 100,000 for listed contraventions, unlimited for serious breaches via court). Amended 2022-2025 including private right of action and digital-ID provisions.

legal_instruments.title: ADGM Data Protection Regulations 2021
legal_instruments.summary: GDPR-aligned regulations for ADGM establishments: lawful bases, records, DPIAs, breach notification (72 hours), transfer safeguards and fines up to USD 28 million for the most serious contraventions.

developments.title: Federal PDPL Executive Regulations still pending
developments.summary: The long-awaited Executive Regulations to the federal PDPL remained unissued, leaving the federal regime largely dormant in practice while free-zone regimes continue active enforcement.

developments.title: DIFC updates Data Protection Regulations
developments.summary: DIFC continued iterating its regime with amendments addressing digital identity, AI-related processing and clarified breach-reporting expectations.

penalties: Federal PDPL: administrative penalties to be prescribed by Cabinet decision (pending). DIFC: fines up to USD 100,000 per listed contravention plus court-imposed amounts. ADGM: up to USD 28 million for serious violations.
cross_border: Federal PDPL: transfers to jurisdictions with adequate protection or under contracts/consent (detail awaits Executive Regulations). DIFC/ADGM maintain adequacy lists (including EU, UK) plus SCC-style safeguards.
